Title: ZAXINONE SYNTHASE 2 regulates growth and arbuscular mycorrhizal symbiosis in rice

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Transcript increased in Oszas2 mutant roots under low Pi; repressed by exogenous zaxinone
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transcript increased in Oszas2 mutant roots under low Pi; repressed by exogenous zaxinone
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR expression analysis in WT and oszas2 roots after low Pi and zaxinone treatment
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Expression is elevated in oszas2 mutants and reduced by zaxinone, consistent with increased SL biosynthesis when zaxinone is deficient
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsD27 transcript abundance increased in oszas2 mutant roots under low Pi. 2. Exogenous zaxinone repressed OsD27 expression back toward WT level. 3. The gene was monitored as part of the SL biosynthetic response downstream of OsZAS2/zaxinone status.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Regulates tillering, rhizosphere signaling, and AM-related interactions
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful marker/target for SL homeostasis modulation alongside zaxinone pathway engineering
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not a primary experimental subject; included because it was directly quantified as a downstream metabolic gene in the Results.

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Cytochrome P450/MAX1 homolog involved in SL biosynthesis

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: AM-inducible in colonized roots; used as marker of arbuscule presence and colonization level
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: AM-inducible in colonized roots; used as marker of arbuscule presence and colonization level
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR during mycorrhization time course and in oszas2 colonization assays
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Lower transcript levels in oszas2 mutants indicate reduced AM colonization
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsPT11 abundance marked arbuscule presence at 21 dpi. 2. OsPT11 transcript was significantly lower in oszas2 mutants during colonization assays. 3. It served as a molecular marker supporting impaired AM symbiosis in the mutant.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Essential macronutrient for plant growth; AM symbiosis enhances phosphate acquisition
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful marker for evaluating AM-mediated phosphate uptake in zaxinone/SL pathway engineering
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: This gene is an AM marker rather than the focal metabolism gene of the study.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Carotenoid cleavage dioxygenase (CCD) family; ZAS CCD subfamily
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cleaves apo-10'-zeaxanthinal at the C13,C14 double bond to form zaxinone and a predicted C9-dialdehyde
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control point for root zaxinone homeostasis; not explicitly established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Predominantly plastid; weaker signal also observed at plasma membrane
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed mainly in roots; strongly induced by low phosphate; enriched toward primary and crown root tips; strong signal in exodermis; in mycorrhizal roots specifically expressed in arbusculated cells and upregulated at 21 dpi
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed mainly in roots; strongly induced by low phosphate; enriched toward primary and crown root tips; strong signal in exodermis; in mycorrhizal roots specifically expressed in arbusculated cells and upregulated at 21 dpi
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native OsZAS2 promoter (1.2 kb) in pOsZAS2::GUS reporter; CaMV 35S for transient localization
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Low phosphate (Pi starvation); arbuscular mycorrhizal colonization; exogenous zaxinone treatment
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: In vitro enzyme assay with recombinant MBP-OsZAS2; in vivo E. coli activity assay; UHPLC and LC-MS product confirmation with synthetic standard; CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; metabolite profiling of roots and exudates; promoter-GUS; in situ hybridization; transient subcellular localization
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes zaxinone biosynthesis in rice roots; loss of function lowers root zaxinone under normal conditions, elevates strigolactone biosynthesis/exudation, impairs growth and tillering, and reduces arbuscular mycorrhizal colonization
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsZAS2 encodes a zaxinone-forming CCD that specifically cleaves apo-10'-zeaxanthinal to zaxinone. 2. CRISPR knockout reduced root zaxinone content by up to 45% under normal conditions and caused shorter roots and shoots, lower biomass, fewer tillers, and reduced grain weight per plant. 3. OsZAS2 is root expressed, induced by low Pi, and in mycorrhizal roots is specifically expressed in arbusculated cells. 4. Oszas2 mutants accumulated higher canonical and noncanonical strigolactones and showed stronger Striga germination activity. 5. Exogenous zaxinone repressed SL biosynthetic gene expression and partially rescued mutant growth defects.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of function causes severe growth retardation, reduced tillering, reduced shoot biomass, and reduced grain weight per plant; manipulation may also alter SL-dependent interactions including Striga stimulation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Growth-promoting apocarotenoid that helps maintain strigolactone homeostasis and supports proper arbuscular mycorrhizal symbiosis
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Manipulating OsZAS2 expression could be used to modulate rice architecture and improve AM symbiosis, but altered SL levels and potential effects on parasitic weed stimulation should be considered
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Under low Pi, root zaxinone levels in oszas2 mutants were unchanged despite elevated SLs, likely due to compensatory upregulation of OsZAS; OsZAS1b and OsZAS1c were not upregulated. The paper also indicates alternative ZAS-independent zaxinone biosynthetic routes may exist.
